summary: Epic Dark Star suite, gritty Row Jimmy, tight rhythm throughout
blurb: The show shines with an unforgettable Dark Star → Eyes Of The World → China Doll sequence, complemented by a fiery Row Jimmy and solid Playing In The Band. Jerry’s down‑and‑dirty lead guitar and Phil’s booming bass drive the second set, while Keith provides crisp piano throughout.
review:
On February 15, 1973 at Dane County Coliseum, the Dead delivered one of their quintessential early‑’73 performances. The first set roared through a punchy mix of folk‑rock staples—Loose Lucy, Beat It On Down The Line, and a seamless China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider transition—setting up a high‑energy launch into Row Jimmy, where Jerry’s gritty lead cut through with soaring solos. 

The second set is the show’s crown jewel: a monumental Dark Star → Eyes Of The World → China Doll suite that reviewers call “epic” and “masterpiece,” featuring Phil’s juicy bass solo and Bill’s crisp tom work. Here Comes Sunshine, El Paso, and a tight Playing In The Band keep the momentum rolling into a joyous encore of Uncle John’s Band and One More Saturday Night. Keith’s piano is a perfect accompaniment throughout, and Donna’s backing vocals add extra sparkle. For anyone chasing that classic 1973 magic, this show delivers it in spades.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Five of seven recordings are strongly positive, repeatedly praising the Dark Star → Eyes Of The World → China Doll suite, Jerry’s gritty lead work, and Phil’s powerful bass solo. Consistent acclaim across multiple sources warrants a high positive sentiment adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Crisp soundboard capture with clear balance, showcasing the epic Dark Star suite and tight band interplay.

key_highlights: Monumental Dark Star → Eyes Of The World → China Doll sequence that defines the show
Gritty, down‑and‑dirty Jerry Garcia solo on Row Jimmy and Dark Star
Phil Lesh’s powerful bass zone and standout solo during the Dark Star suite
Seamless transitions such as China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider

band_performance.Jerry: Gritty, down‑and‑dirty lead guitar with soaring solos, especially on Dark Star and Row Jimmy.
band_performance.Phil: Powerful bass presence with an epic solo in the Dark Star/Eyes segment; juicy moments highlighted in matrix tape.
band_performance.Bob: Strong rhythm work and tasteful fills, notably on Playing In The Band and Casey Jones.
band_performance.Keith: Perfect accompaniment on piano throughout, providing clear, crisp support to the band’s dynamics.
band_performance.Billy: Precise tom tuning, crisp cymbal accents, and overall drumming excellence that drives the jams.
